perturbation.original_text: MORPHO shall pay all charges under this Agreement, including the total Annual Maintenance Charge, within thirty (30) days after receipt of a valid invoice from XIMAGE. Thereafter, the then applicable Annual Maintenance Charge shall be invoiced to, and paid by MORPHO prior to the beginning of the next annual maintenance period. All other charges under this Agreement shall be invoiced by XIMAGE and shall be due and payable within thirty (30) days after receipt of the invoice.
perturbation.changed_text: MORPHO shall pay all charges under this Agreement, including the total Annual Maintenance Charge, within forty-five (45) days after receipt of a valid invoice from XIMAGE. Thereafter, the then applicable Annual Maintenance Charge shall be invoiced to, and paid by MORPHO prior to the beginning of the next annual maintenance period. All other charges under this Agreement shall be invoiced by XIMAGE and shall be due and payable within thirty (30) days after receipt of the invoice.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the payment deadline for all charges under the agreement. The first sentence states that payment is due within forty-five (45) days after receipt of a valid invoice, while the last sentence states that all other charges are due and payable within thirty (30) days. This creates an ambiguity and in-text contradiction in enforcement as it is unclear which deadline applies to which charges, and introduces uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CHARGES The total annual maintenance charge amounts to US $24502 for the first year of maintenance. MORPHO shall pay all charges under this Agreement, including the total Annual Maintenance Charge, within forty-five (45) days after receipt of a valid invoice from XIMAGE. Thereafter, the then applicable Annual Maintenance Charge shall be invoiced to, and paid by MORPHO prior to the beginning of the next annual maintenance period. All other charges under this Agreement shall be invoiced by XIMAGE and shall be due and payable within thirty (30) days after receipt of the invoice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the payment deadline for 'all charges' to 45 days, while retaining the 30-day deadline for 'all other charges.' The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but it highlights the internal inconsistency created by having two different payment deadlines within the same section of the contract. This creates a clear and direct contradiction as to when payment is due, leading to potential disputes and rendering the clause unenforceable due to the ambiguity.

perturbation.original_text: Without prejudice to any other of its rights or remedies, either party may elect to terminate the rights and obligations contained in this Agreement: Page 7 (a) Upon sixty (60) days' written notice if the other party has failed to perform any material obligation required to be performed by it pursuant to this Agreement and such failure has not been cured within such a sixty (60) day period, or (b) Upon sixty (60) days' written notice if the other party has failed to make timely payment of any amounts required to be paid hereunder, or (c) Immediately, (i) if a petition in bankruptcy has been filed by or against the other party, (ii) if the other party has made an assignment for the benefit of creditors, (iii) if a receiver has been appointed or applied for by the other party, or (iv) if the other party has admitted in writing its inability to pay its debts as they become due and payable.
perturbation.changed_text: Without prejudice to any other of its rights or remedies, either party may elect to terminate the rights and obligations contained in this Agreement: Page 7 (a) Upon thirty (30) days' written notice if the other party has failed to perform any material obligation required to be performed by it pursuant to this Agreement and such failure has not been cured within such a sixty (60) day period, or (b) Upon sixty (60) days' written notice if the other party has failed to make timely payment of any amounts required to be paid hereunder, or (c) Immediately, (i) if a petition in bankruptcy has been filed by or against the other party, (ii) if the other party has made an assignment for the benefit of creditors, (iii) if a receiver has been appointed or applied for by the other party, or (iv) if the other party has admitted in writing its inability to pay its debts as they become due and payable.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the written notice time required to terminate the rights and obligations contained in the agreement. Section (a) has been changed to thirty (30) days, but Section (b) remained as sixty (60) days, so this is an in-text contradiction due to the difference of term times between both Sections, creating an ambiguity in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: TERMINATION Without prejudice to any other of its rights or remedies, either party may elect to terminate the rights and obligations contained in this Agreement: Page 7 (a) Upon thirty (30) days' written notice if the other party has failed to perform any material obligation required to be performed by it pursuant to this Agreement and such failure has not been cured within such a sixty (60) day period, or (b) Upon sixty (60) days' written notice if the other party has failed to make timely payment of any amounts required to be paid hereunder, or (c) Immediately, (i) if a petition in bankruptcy has been filed by or against the other party, (ii) if the other party has made an assignment for the benefit of creditors, (iii) if a receiver has been appointed or applied for by the other party, or (iv) if the other party has admitted in writing its inability to pay its debts as they become due and payable.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the notice period in Section (a) from sixty (60) days to thirty (30) days while Section (b) remains at sixty (60) days. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. This creates a direct inconsistency within the agreement regarding the required notice period for termination under different circumstances, making it unclear which notice period applies. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' which states that contradictions arise when 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines...This creates in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations.'
